html_content:
INSIGHT: The Single Most Important Thing: Tiger Global has the lowest cost basis among major shareholders at Rs 11.70/share — a +208% gain even at Ola's depressed Rs 36.1 price. This gives Tiger unique flexibility: sell partial OFS for profit, hold the remainder for a potential turnaround. No other major investor has this luxury — SoftBank (-29%) and Alpha Wave (-42%) are underwater.

Tiger Global (Internet Fund III)

Entity: Internet Fund III Pte Ltd
Stake in Ola Electric: 6.03% (222,436,381 shares)
Acquisition Cost: ₹11.70 per share (weighted average)
Current Price: ₹36.1 (May 2026)
Unrealized P&L: ~+208% gain

---

Investment Context

  • Early-stage investor with lowest cost basis among major shareholders
  • Selling part of stake in IPO Offer for Sale
  • Tiger Global has been reducing India exposure across portfolio

---

Significance

1. Deep in the money — Even at depressed ₹36.1, Tiger sits on 3x+ return
2. No urgency to exit fully — Can sell partial and hold remainder
3. Vote of confidence? — If Tiger retains significant stake post-IPO, it signals continued belief in thesis
4. Cost basis context — Other investors at ₹50-113/share are underwater

---

Data Sources

DATA: Source: quARTERLY_FINANCIALS_EXT.csv — Ola EBITDA turned positive: +2.1% (Q3 FY26), +4.5% (Q4 FY26). Revenue stable at Rs 520 Cr with 39K units at Rs 90K blended ASP. Margins at 36% — a 3x improvement from 12% trough.
DATA: Source: PRODUCT_MIX_QUARTERLY.csv — S1 X now 72% of sales mix (Q4 FY26). Blended ASP stabilized at Rs 90,000. Gross profit per unit risen to Rs 32,400 (Rs 90K × 36%). Breakeven lowered to ~15K units/month from ~40K previously.
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv — Gigafactory at 6 GWh, LFP 46100 at $80/kWh, 30% BOM reduction. If scooter sales recover to 50K+/month, operating leverage from restructured cost base would generate significant EBITDA — a potential catalyst for Tiger's remaining stake.
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/offer_document_summary.txt
Internet Fund III Pte Ltd (Tiger Global) held 222,436,381 Equity Shares (6.03% of pre-Offer paid-up Equity Share capital on a fully diluted basis) in Ola Electric as of the DRHP filing. Weighted average acquisition price: Rs 11.70 per share — the lowest cost basis among all major institutional investors.
Context: Pages 18-19: Pre-Offer shareholding and selling shareholders
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/offer_document_summary.txt
Internet Fund III Pte Ltd was listed as an Investor Selling Shareholder in the IPO Offer for Sale, indicating Tiger Global partially exited its stake through the OFS component.
Context: Page 17-18: Offer Size and Selling Shareholders

Connections

### Causes

- Lowest cost basis at Rs 11.70/share — Tiger invested earliest among major holders; current price Rs 36.1 delivers +208% gain even in a depressed market

- Ola's Gigafactory-driven turnaround — EBITDA turning positive (+4.5% Q4 FY26) validates Tiger's thesis that vertical integration creates margin resilience

- Margin paradox thesis — Tiger likely invested based on unit economics improvement; the S1 X + in-house cells story (gross profit/unit +26%) confirms this

### Effects

- Partial OFS with retained stake — Tiger can take profits now while retaining upside; best-positioned for Ola's eventual recovery

- Vote of confidence signal — If Tiger retains significant stake post-IPO, it signals continued belief in restructuring thesis to the market

- Inverse positioning vs SoftBank — Tiger's 3x+ return vs SoftBank's -29% means different exit timelines, different governance preferences

### Amplifiers

- India exposure reduction trend — Tiger Global has been reducing India portfolio exposure overall; partial Ola sale fits this pattern

- Permanent capital advantage — Unlike SoftBank's Vision Fund (finite life), Tiger's fund structure allows patient holding; no forced exit pressure

- Cost basis context — Other investors at Rs 50-113/share are underwater; Tiger's low cost basis means it can hold through volatility without margin pressure

### Linked Nodes

- Companies/Ola Electric — Primary investment; 6.03% stake

- Companies/SoftBank Vision Fund — 21.98% at Rs 50.94; contrasting cost and return position

- Companies/Bhavish Aggarwal — Promoter with 36.94%; Tiger's relationship differs from SoftBank's

- Strategy/Product_Mix_Cannibalization — Core of the margin recovery Tiger invested behind

- Technology/Gigafactory_Updates — Key value driver for Ola's turnaround thesis

## Related

- Companies/Ola Electric

- Companies/SoftBank Vision Fund

- Financial/IPO Proceeds
